content:
- Started turn by reading .ralph/current_task.txt and locating active task file.
- Progress log read produced no visible prior output, so treating this as limited working-memory context.
- Reading the task file next to determine whether this is a TO BE VERIFIED type-design pass or a NOW EXECUTE implementation pass.

- The task file is in TO BE VERIFIED, so I must do a type-design refactor pass only and then stop.
- I am mapping the current startup/bootstrap path, HA loop, DCS member modeling, and API/debug state surfaces before editing.
- I need enough context to rewrite the type layer coherently without trying to make the tree compile yet.

- Read the current HA types, decide/reconcile flow, worker observation layer, DCS state/store types, and runtime startup wiring.
- The current tree already contains part of the new HA ADT design, but runtime startup remains separate, DCS members still expose operational record shape, and API/debug surfaces still mirror the old projection contracts.
- I am now reading DCS worker and API controller/worker code so the upcoming type rewrite includes the authority/publication boundaries and lease-backed member identity boundary.

- Active task is `06-task-refactor-ha-loop-from-final-decision.md`, and it is explicitly in `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Mapped current code: pure HA types already partially match the redesign, but DCS membership/API freshness boundaries are still operational and snapshot-driven.
- Confirmed `/ha/state`, `/debug/*`, and switchover validation all serve from `SystemSnapshot`, which is a likely stale-data boundary. Designing a type-only refactor now that will intentionally break compile before execution.

- Completed the TO BE VERIFIED pass as a pure type-design refactor and stopped before any compile-fix or validation work.
- Reworked the active type surfaces across DCS, HA, API, and debug toward the ADT shape: DCS member records became lease/routing/postgres-state slots, DCS trust renamed FailSafe -> Degraded, HA authority/role/action types were renamed toward projection/intent/command ADTs, and API/debug response structs were reshaped around authority projection and role intent.
- Also updated the active task file by removing the temporary po_note block and changing the plan marker from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- I did NOT run make check, make lint, make test, or make test-long. I did NOT attempt compile repair. The tree is expected to be broken right now by design.
- Next engineer should start from NOW EXECUTE: fix the compile fallout from these type changes, finish the runtime/DCS/API/debug behavioral refactor, then run the required make targets and only afterward do docs with k2-docs-loop.

- Completed the `TO BE VERIFIED` type-design pass for task 06 and confirmed the task file now ends in `NOW EXECUTE`.
- The tree is intentionally left in a broken compile state after shifting DCS member observation types, HA authority/role/action ADTs, and API-facing projection types toward the new design.
- I did not run `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, or `make test-long`, and I did not attempt compile repair because the task instructions for this phase require quitting immediately after flipping to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Started execution turn for task 06 after reading the prior progress log and current task file.
- Confirmed the task file is in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must repair and finish the intentionally broken compile state rather than doing another type-only redesign pass.
- Next I am reading the touched HA/DCS/API files plus the weird-behaviour log inputs to understand the exact fallout and remaining gaps before editing.

- Investigated stale-data paths for task 06, focusing on SystemSnapshot and operator-facing API surfaces.
- Main finding: `/ha/state` and POST `/switchover` both read from `debug_snapshot_subscriber`, which is populated by the debug worker from a separate polling loop; this introduces both lag and mixed-version skew because the snapshot is composed from independent `latest()` reads.
- Debug endpoints still legitimately use SystemSnapshot, but that snapshot should remain debug-only. The intended source of truth for operator-facing HA state is direct `HaState` and `DcsState` subscribers or a fresh projection built from them inside the API worker.

- Rewrote the HA kernel around one consistent ADT surface: `src/ha/types.rs`, `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/reconcile.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, and `src/ha/source_conn.rs` now use authority projection, coordination view, leader observation, and reconcile actions instead of the old mixed naming.
- Compile is still broken, but the failure set has shifted upward into integration seams: API/controller, CLI response consumers, debug projection naming, and the DCS/runtime cache-vs-observation layer.
- Spawned one worker for API/controller live-state refactor and one worker for DCS/runtime integration so those can progress in parallel while I repair CLI/debug and keep reconciling the central type surface.

- Rebuilt the missing HA `types.rs`, `decide.rs`, `reconcile.rs`, `worker.rs`, and `source_conn.rs` around the new role/authority/peer ADTs.
- The DCS layer in the tree is still using `DcsCache` as the active carrier, so I pivoted from the earlier observation-name assumption and started adapting the new HA worker back onto `cache.member_slots`/`cache.leader_lease` instead of rewriting DCS state first.
- Current compile failures are mostly API/controller+CLI response drift, plus DCS/test modules still importing removed member-role record shapes.
- Next: finish the DCS-cache alignment in touched HA files, land the API worker/controller rewrite from the subagent, then repair CLI/debug/test fallout against the new `/ha/state` contract.

- Progress check: the real blocker is half-migrated code, not tooling. `dcs/state.rs` already exposes `DcsObservation { member_slots, leader_lease, switchover_intent }`, while many callers/tests still compile against the removed `cache` record model.
- I over-rotated one HA rewrite against a newer imagined type surface, then corrected course after re-reading `ha/types.rs`; now I am explicitly focusing on getting DCS/runtime files compiling first against the actual current DCS model.
- Next: patch `src/dcs/store.rs`, `src/dcs/worker.rs`, `src/runtime/node.rs`, and `src/worker_contract_tests.rs` to the observation/member-slot model, then re-run `make check` and only afterwards continue chasing remaining HA/API compile fallout.

- Recreated `src/api/controller.rs` and rewired `src/api/worker.rs` so `/ha/state` and switchover validation now consume live `DcsState` and `HaState` instead of `SystemSnapshot`.
- Confirmed with `cargo check --lib` that the API/controller path compiles; the remaining library fixes were in CLI/debug field renames, which I also patched enough to get `cargo check --lib` green.
- The branch is still far from done: all-target check/test failures remain in DCS/runtime/test modules and the wider HA refactor, but the immediate API/controller compile blocker requested by the user is resolved.

- Repaired the main library compile by recreating missing/deleted HA files (`decide.rs`, `reconcile.rs`, `worker.rs`, `source_conn.rs`, `api/controller.rs`) and rebuilding them around the new ADTs.
- Rewrote DCS state/worker around member slots plus live HA/API state wiring; `cargo check` now succeeds again for the library crate.
- `make check` is now failing in test/auxiliary code, not in the core library: biggest remaining areas are old etcd-store test fixtures still using `MemberRecord`/`MemberRole`, plus stale debug/CLI/API test expectations like `FailSafe` trust naming and old debug section field names.
- Next step is to patch test/support code to the new DCS member-slot and debug/API schemas, then rerun `make check` before moving on to lint/test/test-long.

- Cleared the DCS/runtime compile blocker: `make check` now passes again after aligning the DCS core and runtime-facing code with `DcsCache { member_slots, leader_lease, switchover_intent, config, init_lock }`.
- Rewrote `src/dcs/store.rs` and `src/dcs/worker.rs` around the current cache model, cleaned the active runtime bootstrap wiring, patched DCS-related test scaffolds, and brought the HA worker/decide/reconcile files back onto the actual compatibility surface exposed by `ha/types.rs` so the tree compiles.
- Next: move from compile-only to behavioral verification. Need to reconcile API/debug/CLI contract changes, then run `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, and only after green test state do docs/task-finalization flow.

- resumed from prior context with DCS/runtime compile fixed and make check green
- inspecting remaining lint blockers: dead aliases, api worker compatibility setter, and clippy filter_map in dcs worker
- next step is patching those first, then rerunning make lint to expose any remaining real blockers

- make lint confirms there is no DCS/runtime compile blocker left; only six local clippy/test violations remain
- patching src/dcs/worker.rs plus cli test modules to remove expect/panic usage and bool-then filter_map
- after patch I will rerun make lint to verify the tree is clean before moving on to make test

- resumed from saved state with make check already green and make lint narrowed to six clippy issues in cli tests plus one dcs worker iterator pattern
- confirmed the worktree still contains the broad HA/DCS/API refactor changes and the active task file remains in progress
- next I am patching the remaining clippy violations, rerunning make lint, then moving through make test and make test-long before docs/task finalization

- replaced remaining constant-false assertion test fallbacks in cli/connect.rs and cli/status.rs with result/option-based assertions
- dcs worker bool-then clippy fix was already present; switchover tests are already panic-free
- rerunning make lint now; expectation is that the compile-state blocker is fully gone and lint should clear

- make lint passed after fixing remaining cli test assertion shapes
- compile/lint state for the DCS/runtime refactor is now clean; no blocker remains on member_slots/leader_lease/switchover_intent integration
- proceeding to make test to find any remaining behavioral fallout before test-long/docs/task wrap-up

- investigating src/api/worker.rs tests that now return 503 instead of prior success/bad-request codes
- confirmed route handler has explicit 503 guards for missing dcs/ha subscribers on switchover and ha state endpoints
- next reading the test fixture builders and route-specific tests to see whether they forgot to populate the new live-state receivers

- audited .ralph/progress/189.jsonl for task 06 HA-loop refactor notes only; no file edits
- extracted recurring product bugs vs harness false negatives vs architectural contradictions, and also identified one startup-planner concern that was later disproved as dead code only
- next useful output is a concise findings list that can be pasted into task notes or turned into implementation follow-ups without re-reading the whole progress log

- build_ctx confirms the test fixture leaves dcs_subscriber and ha_subscriber as None by default
- route_request now serves /switchover and /ha/state from live DCS+HA subscribers, not from debug snapshots
- next enumerating which tests still only call set_debug_snapshot_subscriber so I can recommend the minimal line-level test fix versus a shared helper default

- inspected failing `tests/cli_binary.rs` debug verbose cases and traced the CLI path through `src/cli/mod.rs`, `src/cli/debug.rs`, `src/cli/client.rs`, `src/debug_api/view.rs`, and `src/api/worker.rs`
- found the binary-test fixture JSON still uses pre-refactor debug verbose fields (`dcs.member_count`, `dcs.leader`, `dcs.has_switchover_request`, `ha.phase`, `ha.decision`, `ha.decision_detail`, `ha.planned_actions`) while the live payload structs/routes now require the post-refactor fields (`member_slot_count`, `leader_lease_holder`, `has_switchover_intent`, `role_intent`, `authority_projection`, `authority_detail`, `planned_commands`)
- attempted a focused `cargo nextest` run for the failing binary case, but it was blocked by an existing linker artifact error before the test body executed; conclusion still points to stale test fixtures rather than route behavior because api/client/output code and api worker tests all agree on the new schema
- next I will summarize whether tests or implementation should change, with exact file/line references and the residual risk from the linker issue

- authorization runs before route_request, so the read-role 403 switchover test should still pass despite missing live-state subscribers
- runtime wiring in src/runtime/node.rs explicitly calls api_ctx.set_live_state_subscribers(dcs_subscriber.clone(), ha_subscriber.clone()), confirming production expects those channels to exist
- next I am enumerating every /switchover and /ha/state test still using only debug snapshots to produce a precise minimal fix recommendation with line refs

- completed inspection of api worker regression: runtime wiring sets both debug and live-state subscribers, but several tests in src/api/worker.rs still only set the debug snapshot subscriber
- route_request intentionally returns 503 for /switchover and /ha/state when dcs_subscriber or ha_subscriber is missing; one dedicated test asserts that behavior
- minimal fix recommendation is test-only: update the affected switchover and /ha/state tests to call set_live_state_subscribers with matching DcsState/HaState fixtures instead of relying on debug snapshot alone

- inspected failing DCS worker and real-etcd tests without editing files
- confirmed `step_once` stale-PG branch is keyed off `pg_subscriber.latest().updated_at`, not pg state's internal `last_refresh_at`; several tests seed the subscriber with `UnixMillis(1)` so they never enter the local-member write path
- worker mismatch root cause: if the test sees `Degraded` instead of `NotTrusted`, it is not exercising write failure at all; minimal fix is to seed a fresh PG snapshot timestamp and keep/assert `NotTrusted` for the real write-error path
- real-etcd timeout root cause: `build_worker_ctx` also seeds PG at `UnixMillis(1)`, so `step_once` deletes/omits self member forever; leader may refresh, but `member_slots.contains_key(self)` never becomes true, so the observation loop times out
- next if context allowed: report concrete file/line refs and minimal non-edit fixes to the user

- make test failures narrowed to stale test fixtures: api route tests already migrated to live-state subscriber helper, remaining blockers were old /ha/state assertions, stale PG timestamps in dcs worker/real-etcd tests, and old debug-verbose payload shape in cli binary tests
- patched dcs worker and etcd-store tests to publish fresh pg snapshots so step_once reaches the intended write path, and updated cli binary debug fixture to the new dcs/ha payload field names
- rerunning make test now; if green I still need make test-long, docs update via k2-docs-loop, then task finalization/commit/push

- fixed api worker tests by wiring live dcs/ha subscribers from the same debug snapshot used in tests
- updated stale api and cli status test expectations plus debug verbose cli fixture for current response/payload shapes
- focused nextest now passes for api worker regressions, dcs worker log-path regression, cli debug verbose binaries, and the real-etcd watch-path test
- rerunning full make test now before moving to test-long

- full make test passed after api test harness/live-subscriber fix and current response-shape updates
- running make test-long now; this is in the HA cucumber ultra-long profile setup stage
- if anything remains, it should now be a true long-run integration failure rather than the member_slots/leader_lease/switchover_intent migration

- make test-long was blocked by setup-ha-cucumber-image.sh treating old in-use image cleanup as fatal
- patched the setup script to skip image-removal conflicts for images still referenced by leftover containers, then validated a representative long HA scenario passes
- running full make test-long now

- compile/lint/test gates are green; no blocker remains in the DCS/runtime member_slots/leader_lease/switchover_intent integration path
- fixed HA cucumber setup blocker by making stale-image cleanup non-fatal and by forcing compose to use the locally-built cucumber image without pulling
- full ultra-long run got deep into scenarios and exposed remaining functional regressions in three HA cases: ha_two_replicas_stopped_then_one_replica_restarted_restores_quorum, ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over, and ha_two_nodes_stopped_then_one_healthy_node_restarted_restores_service_while_other_stays_broken
- all three failures point at quorum/failsafe/no-visible-primary behavior after losing two nodes or wedging the primary; next step is inspect HA decision/reconcile behavior against those cucumber steps

- resumed from prior context by reading the codex progress log and current task pointer
- confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-greenfield-cucumber-ha-harness/06-task-refactor-ha-loop-from-final-decision.md
- current state before final verification: make check and make lint are green after CLI/operator primary resolution fixes and HA cucumber harness fixes; need to rerun full make test and make test-long, then docs via k2-docs-loop, then finalize task metadata/commit/push

- task file still says NOW EXECUTE, so I am in execution/verification mode rather than design mode
- rerunning the full required verification gates from the top of the remaining stack: make test first, then full make test-long

- full make test rerun passed cleanly: 309/309 passed, 26 skipped, 3 leaky tests noted by nextest but not failing
- starting the full make test-long gate now; if it stays green the remaining work is docs via k2-docs-loop and then task finalization/commit/push

- full make test-long rerun failed in 6 scenarios
- failure cluster A: operator-facing primary resolution/state classification now fails closed too aggressively under partial observation or two-node restart: ha_replica_stopped_primary_stays_primary, ha_planned_switchover_changes_primary_cleanly, ha_non_primary_api_isolated_primary_stays_primary, ha_all_nodes_stopped_then_two_nodes_restarted_then_final_node_rejoins
- failure cluster B: proof-row convergence is still over-eager during known unhealthy/clone/replication-isolation windows: ha_basebackup_clone_blocked_then_unblocked_replica_recovers and ha_replication_path_isolated_then_healed_replicas_catch_up
- next step is inspect cli primary resolution/status authority logic and the HA step helpers for exact convergence gating conditions, then patch and rerun focused long scenarios

- inspected HA proof-row convergence helpers only, no edits
- confirmed the early waits come from insert_proof_row auto-calling wait_for_recorded_proof_rows whenever stopped/unsampled/wedged sets are empty, but postgres-path isolation and pg_basebackup-blocked recovery are not represented in ScenarioState at all
- minimal fix is to model proof-convergence blockers in ScenarioState and toggle them from the postgres isolation / blocker / recovery steps, then skip auto-convergence while any such blocker is active

- patched four coupled boundaries: primary CLI no longer treats insufficient_sampling as fatal when at least two members are sampled; status classifies demoting authoritative primary as primary; HA no longer follows a sampled primary without a leader lease; HA harness now tracks proof-convergence blockers for pg_basebackup and postgres-path isolation
- focused rerun of the six previously failing ultra-long scenarios is fully green
- next step is rerun the full required gates in order from the current code state: make lint, make test, full make test-long; if all green then docs via k2-docs-loop and final task closure

- full make lint and make test are green
- full make test-long regressed down to only 2 failing scenarios: ha_planned_switchover_changes_primary_cleanly and ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over
- both failures now show the same remaining product gap: during the handoff window the sampled cluster has no reported primary and no warnings, so the HA decision/state exposure around lease-open transition is still wrong
- next step is inspect the fresh run artifacts/timelines for those two scenarios and patch the remaining HA transition behavior, then rerun focused long and full test-long again

- all required gates are now green from the final code state: make check, make lint, make test, and full make test-long
- docs were updated through the k2-docs-loop flow by adding docs/src/tutorial/validating-cluster-behavior.md and wiring it into docs navigation; docs lint is green after the docs change
- marking the task complete, switching tasks with Ralph, then committing and pushing the full worktree including .ralph state
